what_it_does: A stdlib-only HTTP server that serves the operator canvas/studio UI and exposes a broad REST and SSE API over the shared Company Suite and store. It routes reads and writes for graphs, mockups, cognition, voice, decisions, tools, projections, activation, and self-change audit, and hosts several background daemons.
observations: BRIDGE_ROUTES is declared as a single-source route table used by capabilities() and a drift-gate test.
Static assets include canvas index.html, /studio redirect, /design-system.css, and mockup HTML files with optional studio click-capture injection.
Voice and chat streaming reuse a shared _stream_parts driver with a producer/consumer overlap pattern.
A fail-closed tool-invoke gate mirrors remote_exposure.json with operator-face overlays and posture checks.
Background daemon threads warm the vector cache, drain the commit queue, and reconcile extraction indexes.
The projection engine supports semantic, separator, and nucleation lenses plus scale rungs, MRL truncation, and binary quantization.
Cognition endpoints wrap the shared runtime.cognition engine for run_role, run_items, and run_reduce.
Operator-session tokens are minted but enforcement is intentionally disabled behind env flags.
standouts: BRIDGE_ROUTES single-source design with explicit drift-gate commentary.
_stream_parts concurrent brain/TTS overlap reused for voice and chat streaming.
_tool_gate fail-closed operator invoke gate with posture, confirm, and allowed-op checks.
build_projection universal projection dispatch with scale/MRL/binary axes.
Background commit-queue and freshness daemons hosted inside the bridge process.
conventions: Extensive inline comments explaining design rationale, cross-references, and safety boundaries.
Lazy imports inside functions to avoid import-time side effects and circular imports.
sys.path manipulation to reach ops/cli modules from local functions.
Global SUITE instantiated at module level and shared by all handlers.
Nested helper functions and closures capturing mutable boxes for streaming state.
Long if/elif route dispatch chains in do_GET and do_POST.
Manual parse_qs flattening and repeated query-string parsing in some handlers.
concerns: Very large module combining HTTP serving, streaming orchestration, projection math, tool gating, and background daemons.
Broad except Exception in do_GET/do_POST returns 400 with a generic message, potentially masking real bugs.
Background daemon loops silently swallow all exceptions with bare pass.
Operator token enforcement is a scaffold with a TODO; the safety boundary is not active.
Nested streaming functions depend on socket state, making unit testing difficult.
Some handlers shadow or re-parse the query string, increasing risk of inconsistent q values.
notes: This is the monolithic operator-facing HTTP surface for the Company runtime, bridging the shared Suite to the canvas and studio while hosting long-lived background daemons and a fail-closed tool gate.
questions: How exactly does tests/bridge_routes_acceptance.py verify the route table drift?
What is the precise shape of the final part dict returned by Suite.chat_parts for proposal/action/thread_id/history?
How does the MCP face lazily import this module without circular import issues?
What security guarantees are expected once operator-token enforcement is enabled?
How are background daemon failures surfaced if they silently pass on exceptions?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated as a thin HTTP UI face over the shared Suite; in practice it is a large monolithic dispatcher that also contains a projection engine, streaming orchestrator, tool gate, and background daemons, but still serves the single-system goal.
apparent_themes: Operator-only safety boundaries versus the MCP/agent face
intention_signals: The file is trying to be the unified human-operated control plane for a multi-face system: one HTTP surface that exposes everything the operator needs while reusing the same Suite, store, and cognition engine the agent face uses, with explicit gating to prevent accidental autonomous writes.
novelty: The combination of a single-source route table with a drift gate, a reusable streaming parts driver used for both voice and text, and a universal projection engine with Matryoshka and binary-quantization zoom axes inside a stdlib-only HTTP server is unusual.
contribution: A stdlib-only HTTP operator surface that centralizes UI route dispatch, static asset serving, streaming voice/chat/builder endpoints, a fail-closed tool-invoke gate, projection helpers, and long-lived background daemons over the shared Suite and store.
summary_for_embedding: The operator-facing HTTP bridge for the Company runtime, exposing REST and SSE endpoints for graphs, cognition, voice, decisions, tools, projections, and the mockup studio over a shared Suite.
intention_for_embedding: To give the human operator a single, safe, always-up web surface that reuses the same runtime brain and substrate as the agent face while gating consequential actions.

symbols.does: Validate and resolve a mockup filename to a path inside MOCKUPS_DIR.
symbols.description: Checks that the supplied name is a bare .html basename and that its realpath stays within the mockups directory, raising ValueError on traversal or invalid names.

symbols.does: Return the JSONL feedback path for a mockup, path-safe.
symbols.description: Validates the mockup filename and resolves design/mockups/.feedback/<mockup>.jsonl, raising ValueError if the name would escape FEEDBACK_DIR.

symbols.does: Read a mockup's feedback thread as a list of entries.
symbols.description: Opens the matching JSONL file and returns parsed entries oldest-first, skipping malformed lines with a stderr warning.

symbols.does: Build the studio gallery corpus from disk plus curated meta.
symbols.description: Lists every .html mockup under design/mockups except STUDIO.html, joins each to _CORPUS_META, and returns sorted records with derived defaults for uncurated files.

symbols.does: Set a model's context window and auto-size GPU utilization.
symbols.description: Updates max_model_len in the service registry, computes gpu_util from the model's _profile if available, and restarts the service only if it fits within the GPU budget.

symbols.does: Find the active local brain service key for co-residence sizing.
symbols.description: Returns the registry key of the running brain service whose configured model matches the rhm_config model, or None for cloud or ollama brains.

symbols.does: Resolve a TTS engine name to its service base URL.
symbols.description: Maps kokoro or an absent engine to the env-overridable TTS_URL, maps known alternate engines to localhost ports, and raises ValueError for unknown engines.

symbols.does: Drive a chat_parts generator with brain/TTS overlap.
symbols.description: Runs a producer thread that pulls parts ahead while the consumer synthesizes and emits each sentence, honoring cancellation and re-raising brain errors on the consumer thread.

symbols.does: Producer thread inside _stream_parts.
symbols.description: Pulls the next part from the generator only when the client is still connected, puts parts on a queue, and records the final part before sending a sentinel.

symbols.does: Background vector-index cache warm.
symbols.description: Calls SUITE.store.warm_vector_cache once in a daemon thread at startup to avoid cold first semantic queries; failures are isolated.

symbols.does: Background FIFO drainer for the shared commit queue.
symbols.description: Runs every ~2 seconds in a daemon thread, invoking ops.commit_queue.drain under a cross-process flock to serialize git commits; transient errors are ignored.

symbols.does: Background auto-reindexer for extraction assets.
symbols.description: Polls mtime of extractions JSONL files every ~10 minutes and reconciles the vector index when assets change; failures are swallowed.

symbols.does: Snapshot extractions asset mtimes.
symbols.description: Returns a dict of mtime_ns values for the tracked extraction asset files.

symbols.does: Mint a per-process operator session token.
symbols.description: Generates a URL-safe token, stores it in the process set, and returns it for the surface to attach to consequential requests.

symbols.does: Check whether a token was minted by this process.
symbols.description: Returns True if the supplied token is present in the in-process _OPERATOR_TOKENS set; this is a runaway-prevention check, not adversary-proof.

symbols.does: Lazy-bind the FastMCP tool manager to this bridge's Suite.
symbols.description: Imports mcp_face.server on first use and calls build_mcp(SUITE), caching the resulting tool manager so no second Suite is constructed.

symbols.does: Load the lead-owned tool metadata registry.
symbols.description: Reads mcp_face/tool_meta.json fresh per call and returns the tools map; degrades to {} if the file is missing or malformed.

symbols.does: Load the remote exposure posture allow-list.
symbols.description: Reads mcp_face/remote_exposure.json fresh per call; raises RuntimeError if missing or malformed to enforce fail-closed behavior.

symbols.does: Load operator-face posture overlay.
symbols.description: Reads mcp_face/tool_operator_overlay.json and returns the tools map; degrades to {} if absent or malformed.

symbols.does: Compute effective operator-face posture for a tool.
symbols.description: Prefers the overlay's operator_posture if present, otherwise falls back to remote_exposure.json's remote_posture, returning an empty string if unknown.

symbols.does: Fail-closed gate for generic tool invocation.
symbols.description: Checks explicitly_denied list, deny-by-default, posture allowances, hazard confirm, and allowed-op whitelist; returns a tuple of allowed, reason, and posture.

symbols.does: Decide whether a tool posture implies a write.
symbols.description: Returns True for postures other than safe or design, indicating the tool-run should require operator attribution.

symbols.does: Semantic meaning-field projection with scale/MRL/binary axes.
symbols.description: Resolves vectors for items or theme centroids, applies optional Matryoshka truncation and binary quantization, and calls project() for the circle or a coarse rung view.

symbols.does: Apply MRL truncation and optional binary quantization.
symbols.description: Truncates a vector to the requested dimension and, if binary quantization is enabled, maps each component to plus or minus one.

symbols.does: Attach scale ladder and active binding metadata to a projection body.
symbols.description: Adds scale, space, emb, res, and quant fields so the frontend can render the zoom ladder and know the active representation.

symbols.does: Resolve a centre vector portably across unit and theme spaces.
symbols.description: Looks up the centre in the unit space, a cluster:// theme's native rung space, or the current coarse rung space, returning None if absent.

symbols.does: Enrich coarse theme points with scale metadata.
symbols.description: Adds size, exemplar, member count, and child rung information to each projected theme point.

symbols.does: Two-gravity separator projection.
symbols.description: Resolves two pole vectors and every item vector in a lens, applies MRL/binary transforms, and calls project() with poles to produce a signed lean field.

symbols.does: MRL plus binary transform for separator vectors.
symbols.description: Truncates vectors and optionally binarizes them consistently for pole and item comparisons.

symbols.does: Resolve a pole reference to its vector.
symbols.description: Fetches a unit or anchor vector from the lens space or a cluster:// theme centroid from its rung space.

symbols.does: Type-nucleation projection using the 20/80 water law.
symbols.description: Loads type centroids from a scale pyramid, computes admission radii from member cosines, types content items, and calls project() with a nucleation report plus available picker metadata.

symbols.does: MRL plus binary transform for nucleation vectors.
symbols.description: Truncates type and item vectors to the requested dimension and optionally sign-quantizes them before cosine comparison.

symbols.does: Cosine similarity helper.
symbols.description: Computes the cosine of two equal-length numeric vectors using the square root of dot products.

symbols.does: Universal projection resolver dispatching by binding radius_from.
symbols.description: Discovers bindings, loads events, and routes to semantic, separator, nucleation, or pure project() based on the binding's radius_from and angle_from.

symbols.does: Emit a cognition telemetry event through the Suite.
symbols.description: Wraps SUITE._emit with a summary derived from the payload, used as the emit sink for run_items and run_reduce.

symbols.does: Resolve a role id or draft fields to a Role object.
symbols.description: Looks up registered roles or renders and loads a draft field-set via the authoring path; fails loud for unknown ids.

symbols.does: Generate a face-tagged turn identifier.
symbols.description: Returns a string combining the prefix, wall-clock time, and monotonic millis for unique run addresses.

symbols.does: Run one role and persist/index its output.
symbols.description: Resolves the role, builds context including address-valued inputs, calls _cog.run_role, stores the output at run://<turn>/<role>, and emits an op.run record.

symbols.does: Fan one role over multiple units.
symbols.description: Resolves the role and delegates to _cog.run_items, returning per-unit addresses, finish order, skipped items, and wall time.

symbols.does: Reduce multiple run outputs into one.
symbols.description: Dispatches to _cog.run_reduce in role, rule, or cluster mode, validating the reduce rule name and role resolution before the call.

symbols.does: Create the first-run demo graph if absent.
symbols.description: Saves a small codebase graph with question, codebase, and answer nodes wired together.

symbols.does: HTTP request handler class for the bridge.
symbols.description: Subclass of BaseHTTPRequestHandler that dispatches GET and POST routes, serves static assets, and implements SSE, chat, voice, and Claude Code streaming endpoints.

symbols.does: Send an HTTP response with code, body, and content type.
symbols.description: Encodes the body, sets Content-Length, writes headers, and sends the payload.

symbols.does: Read and parse the JSON request body.
symbols.description: Reads Content-Length bytes from rfile and returns the parsed JSON object.

symbols.does: Flatten parsed query string to first value per key.
symbols.description: Takes a ParseResult and returns a dict mapping each query key to its first value.

symbols.does: Dispatch all GET routes.
symbols.description: Long if/elif chain handling static pages, mockups, images, SSE stream, and the many /api read endpoints; catches exceptions and returns 400 JSON.

symbols.does: Check whether a decision is currently decided.
symbols.description: Uses the decision registry and compose_state to verify a decision.decided event still reflects an authoritative decided state.

symbols.does: Probe a TTS engine's liveness and voice list.
symbols.description: GETs /voices from the engine base URL and returns up, voices, and default info, swallowing errors to report up:false.

symbols.does: Server-Sent Events endpoint for the shared event log.
symbols.description: Sends text/event-stream data for events since the cursor or Last-Event-ID, with periodic keepalive heartbeats, until the client disconnects.

symbols.does: Text-only streaming chat turn endpoint.
symbols.description: Drives SUITE.chat_parts through _stream_parts with no audio, emits per-part NDJSON events, captures the final part for proposal/action/thread state, and emits a done event.

symbols.does: Write an NDJSON event to the chat stream.
symbols.description: Serializes an object to JSON with a newline, flushes, and sets the gone flag on broken pipe.

symbols.does: Probe whether the chat client disconnected.
symbols.description: Uses select plus MSG_PEEK to detect an EOF on the socket and updates the gone flag.

symbols.does: Split text into sentences for the chat stream contract.
symbols.description: Splits on sentence-ending punctuation whitespace; returns the whole text if no sentences are found.

symbols.does: Tee the parts generator and capture the final part.
symbols.description: Yields each part while storing the final part in a box so the done event can read proposal, action, thread_id, and history.

symbols.does: Emit a per-part text display event.
symbols.description: Sends a type:part NDJSON line with an incrementing index and the part text.

symbols.does: Builder side-panel Claude Code turn streaming endpoint.
symbols.description: Reads prompt, optional session id, address, context block, and pointables; runs run_turn and emits init, text, tool, done, and error NDJSON events.

symbols.does: Write an NDJSON event to the Claude stream.
symbols.description: Serializes and flushes an event, marking the stream gone on write failure.

symbols.does: Probe whether the Claude stream client disconnected.
symbols.description: Select plus MSG_PEEK check used by run_turn to abort if the operator closes the connection.

symbols.does: Streaming voice turn endpoint.
symbols.description: Transcribes audio, drives chat_parts through _stream_parts with TTS synthesis, emits transcript, part, chunk, reply, and done NDJSON events, records trial turns and durable voice logs.

symbols.does: Write an NDJSON event to the voice stream.
symbols.description: Serializes and flushes an event, setting gone on broken pipe.

symbols.does: Probe whether the voice client disconnected.
symbols.description: Select plus MSG_PEEK EOF detection used to cancel speculative synthesis promptly.

symbols.does: Split a part into sentences for TTS.
symbols.description: Same regex sentence split used for voice synthesis ordering.

symbols.does: Synthesize one sentence to WAV bytes.
symbols.description: Calls voice_loop.speak for the selected engine and voice and records the elapsed milliseconds.

symbols.does: Emit one audio chunk event.
symbols.description: Base64-encodes the WAV bytes and sends a type:chunk NDJSON line with index, text, wav_b64, and milliseconds.

symbols.does: Emit a completed part event for the voice stream.
symbols.description: Sends a type:part NDJSON line; the index is currently a placeholder value of -1.

symbols.does: Dispatch all POST routes.
symbols.description: Long if/elif chain handling brain ask, decision explain/update/propose, channel post, tool invoke, voice/chat/Claude streaming, graph edits, cognition runs, and many other write endpoints; catches exceptions and returns 400 JSON.

symbols.does: Suppress default access logging.
symbols.description: Overrides BaseHTTPRequestHandler.log_message to do nothing, keeping the console quiet.
